Coverage Matrix
How to read the matrix
Each cell = one source × event pair
3+0.62 Big number = articles scraped for this cell
2−0.45 Small number = average stance (−1 to +1)
— Dash = searched, nothing found
· Dot = not searched yet
Cell colour = predicted direction
Green — bullish / positive outcome predicted
Red — bearish / negative outcome predicted
Grey — neutral / ambiguous
FALSE badge shown only when event did not happen
Stance scale (−1 to +1)
+1.0 — strongly predicts positive/bullish outcome
0.0 — neutral, no directional view
−1.0 — strongly predicts negative/bearish outcome
Column headers are colour-coded by source. Click a cell to jump to its time-series chart.

Event Time Series
How to read the charts
Axes
X-axis — days before the event (right edge = day of event, further left = earlier)
Left Y-axis — stance scaled to −100 → +100
  +100 = predicted strongly positive outcome
  0 = neutral
  −100 = predicted strongly negative outcome
Right Y-axis — Polymarket probability % (white shaded area, where available)
Elements
Each coloured line = one news source; each dot = one extracted prediction
The white shaded area (if present) = Polymarket contract probability
Hover a dot to see the source quote and certainty
Metrics (predictions table below)
Certainty — how confident the author sounds (0% = heavily hedged → 100% = absolute)
Hedge — share of hedging language ("might", "could", "possibly")
Days Before — how far in advance this prediction was published
